Active Monitoring · Thailand

Where Nature
Is Closing Its Doors

An interactive thesis exploring the closure of Thailand's most beloved natural destinations — and charting a mindful path to rediscover them.

3 Restricted

About WAVS

โครงการวิทยานิพนธ์ที่ติดตามสถานะการปิดอุทยานแห่งชาติทั่วประเทศไทย เพื่อส่งเสริมการท่องเที่ยวเชิงอนุรักษ์อย่างยั่งยืน
A thesis project tracking national park closures across Thailand to promote mindful, sustainable nature tourism.

🗺️
Nationwide Coverage
WAVS monitors all 156 national parks across Thailand's 15 administrative regions — tracking seasonal, temporary, and indefinite closures in real time using official DNP data.
📊
Data-Driven Research
A graduate thesis investigating the environmental and policy conditions behind park closures — why ecosystems close their doors, and what it means for Thailand's relationship with its natural heritage.
🌿
Mindful Alternatives
For every closed site, WAVS identifies open alternatives offering comparable natural experiences with lower ecological impact — so you can still explore Thailand responsibly.

National Parks
Status Today

Closure status for all 156 Thai national parks as of 22 March 2026. Click any pin to see which sites are affected and why. Data: กรมอุทยานแห่งชาติ สัตว์ป่า และพันธุ์พืช (DNP).

8 Temporarily Closed
26 Indefinite Closures
41 Seasonal (active now)
81 Open Today

Latest Updates

Recent announcements from the Department of National Parks and developments in Thailand's protected area management.

Closure 14 March 2026
Khuean Srinagarindra Temporarily Closed Following Fire Risk Assessment
The Department of National Parks has issued a temporary closure order for Khuean Srinagarindra National Park in Kanchanaburi, citing elevated wildfire risk conditions. All visitor entry points and campgrounds are closed until further notice.
DNP Announcement →
Seasonal 1 March 2026
Annual Closures Begin Across 41 National Parks for 2026 Season
As the dry season progresses, 41 national parks across Thailand have entered their scheduled annual closure periods — primarily waterfall trails and high-risk forest zones. Most closures run through May to September 2026.
View Closure List →
Policy 20 January 2026
DNP Launches Centralised Park Monitoring Dashboard
Thailand's Department of National Parks, Wildlife and Plant Conservation (กรมอุทยานแห่งชาติ สัตว์ป่า และพันธุ์พืช) has announced a new centralised digital system to track real-time status across all 156 parks — the first initiative of its kind in Southeast Asia.
Read More →
Research 5 December 2025
Study Links Overtourism to Accelerated Coral Bleaching in Marine Parks
A joint study by Mahidol University and WWF Thailand found that 7 of Thailand's 15 marine national parks show measurable coral bleaching directly correlated with visitor density — cited as key evidence behind several ongoing indefinite closures in the Andaman Sea.
Full Paper →

Sites

Thailand's natural sanctuaries facing environmental pressure from overtourism, ecological damage, and climate stress — with mindful alternatives for each.
